  "text": "The G1 GC concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in sg-evals/jdk--742e735d is implemented across several key C++ source files under src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/.\n\n## 1. G1ConcurrentMark Start Trigger\n\nThe decision to initiate concurrent marking is made in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp`. The method `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()` evaluates heap occupancy against the Initiating Heap Occupancy Percent (IHOP) threshold (tracked in `g1IHOPControl.cpp`). When the threshold is exceeded, `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()` is called, which sets the collector state (`g1CollectorState.cpp`) to `in_concurrent_start_gc`. This triggers the `G1ConcurrentMarkThread` (in `g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`) to begin a new cycle. The actual cycle begins with `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()` (in `g1ConcurrentMark.cpp`), followed by `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()` which performs the actual concurrent tracing.\n\n## 2. SATB Marking Queue Processing\n\nThe Snapshot-At-The-Beginning (SATB) write barrier is implemented in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSet.cpp` — the `G1BarrierSet` class intercepts reference writes via `write_ref_field_pre()` and `write_ref_array_pre_work()`. When the SATB queue is active (during concurrent marking), it enqueues the pre-value into the thread-local SATB queue.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSetRuntime.cpp` — runtime stub entry point `write_ref_field_pre_entry()` for compiled code.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/satbMarkQueue.cpp` — defines the base `SATBMarkQueue` and `SATBMarkQueueSet` classes. Buffers are flushed when full, becoming 'completed buffers' available for processing.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp` — `G1SATBMarkQueueSet` extends the base class with G1-specific filtering (`G1SATBMarkQueueFilterFn`) to exclude already-dead or young-gen references. During the Remark pause, `G1ConcurrentMark.cpp` contains the `G1RemarkThreadsClosure` that flushes per-thread SATB queues.\n\n## 3. Remembered Set Scanning\n\nThe remembered set infrastructure is in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSet.cpp` — central class `G1RemSet` (created in `g1CollectedHeap.cpp`). Key methods: `prepare_for_scan_heap_roots()` sets up `G1RemSetScanState`, and `scan_heap_roots()` performs the actual card scanning to find cross-region references into the collection set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1HeapRegionRemSet.cpp` — per-region remembered set storage.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CardSet.cpp` — the card set data structure backing each region's remembered set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSetTrackingPolicy.cpp` — tracks which regions need remembered set scanning.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentRebuildAndScrub.cpp` — concurrently rebuilds remembered sets after a marking cycle.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1DirtyCardQueue.cpp` — dirty card queue that feeds the concurrent refinement threads (`g1ConcurrentRefine.cpp`) which keep the remembered sets up to date.\n\n## 4. Mixed Collection Region Selection\n\nAfter a concurrent marking cycle completes, old-gen regions become candidates for mixed collections:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetChooser.cpp` — `G1CollectionSetChooser::build()` is called post-marking to scan all old-gen regions, compute their reclaimable bytes (liveness from the marking bitmap), sort them by efficiency (reclaimable bytes per byte of remembered set), and populate the `G1CollectionSetCandidates`.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetCandidates.cpp` — `G1CollectionSetCandidates` maintains the sorted list of candidate old-gen regions (from marking groups and retained regions), provides iteration, and tracks which regions are in the collection set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSet.cpp` — `G1CollectionSet::finalize_old_part()` selects the actual set of old-gen regions for the next mixed GC pause, constrained by the remaining pause time budget. It pulls from the `G1CollectionSetCandidates` in efficiency order.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1YoungCollector.cpp` — `calculate_collection_set()` orchestrates the full collection set construction by calling `finalize_initial_collection_set()` which ultimately calls `finalize_old_part()`.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1YoungGCPostEvacuateTasks.cpp` — after evacuation, post-GC tasks may reorder candidates by efficiency and handle retained regions.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp` — `G1Policy::candidates()` provides access to the collection set candidates."
}

G1ConcurrentMark Start Trigger\n\nThe decision to initiate concurrent marking is made in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp`. The method `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()` evaluates heap occupancy against the Initiating Heap Occupancy Percent (IHOP) threshold (managed in `g1IHOPControl.cpp`). When the threshold is exceeded, `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()` is called, which sets the collector state (via `g1CollectorState.cpp`) to `in_concurrent_start_gc=true` and clears `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible`. This signals the `G1ConcurrentMarkThread` (in `g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`) to begin a new marking cycle. The cycle starts with `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()` followed by `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()` which does the actual concurrent tracing. The `G1ConcurrentMark` object itself is constructed and initialized in `g1CollectedHeap.cpp`. VM operations for initiating concurrent marking safepoints are in `g1VMOperations.cpp`.\n\n## 2. SATB Marking Queue Processing\n\nThe Snapshot-At-The-Beginning (SATB) write barrier is implemented across:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSet.cpp` — `G1BarrierSet::write_ref_array_pre_work()` and related methods intercept reference writes. When the SATB queue is active during concurrent marking, the pre-value of the overwritten reference is enqueued into the thread-local `SATBMarkQueue`.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSetRuntime.cpp` — runtime stub `write_ref_field_pre_entry()` for compiled code paths that invokes `enqueue_known_active()` to log the old reference value.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/satbMarkQueue.cpp` — base `SATBMarkQueue` and `SATBMarkQueueSet` classes. Buffers fill up during mutation and become 'completed buffers' for concurrent processing.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp` — `G1SATBMarkQueueSet` extends the base with G1-specific filtering (`G1SATBMarkQueueFilterFn`) to exclude already-marked or young-gen references. During the Remark STW pause, `g1ConcurrentMark.cpp` contains `G1RemarkThreadsClosure` which flushes per-thread SATB queues so all remaining pre-values are processed before marking concludes.\n\n## 3. Remembered Set Scanning\n\nThe remembered set (remset) infrastructure is implemented in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSet.cpp` — central `G1RemSet` class. `prepare_for_scan_heap_roots()` initializes the `G1RemSetScanState`; `scan_heap_roots()` iterates dirty card ranges and scans for cross-region references pointing into the collection set. The `G1RemSetScanState` inner class manages which regions have dirty cards to scan.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1HeapRegionRemSet.cpp` — per-region remembered set (`G1HeapRegionRemSet`) that tracks which other regions have pointers into a given region.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CardSet.cpp` — the card-set data structure (`G1CardSet`) backing each region's remembered set using a HOWL (Hierarchical Or With Lists) structure.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSetTrackingPolicy.cpp` — decides which regions need remset tracking (`update_at_allocate`, `update_at_free`).\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentRebuildAndScrub.cpp` — concurrently rebuilds and scrubs remembered sets after a marking cycle completes.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1DirtyCardQueue.cpp` — the dirty card queue that feeds concurrent refinement threads (`g1ConcurrentRefine.cpp`) which keep remsets up to date between GC pauses.\n\n## 4.   "text": "The G1 GC concurrent marking and mixed collection cycle in sg-evals/jdk--742e735d is implemented across several key C++ source files under src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/ and src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/.\n\n## 1. G1ConcurrentMark Start Trigger\n\nThe decision to initiate concurrent marking is made in `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp`. The method `G1Policy::need_to_start_conc_mark()` evaluates heap occupancy against the Initiating Heap Occupancy Percent (IHOP) threshold (managed in `g1IHOPControl.cpp`). When the threshold is exceeded, `G1Policy::initiate_conc_mark()` is called, which sets the collector state (via `g1CollectorState.cpp`) to `in_concurrent_start_gc=true` and clears `initiate_conc_mark_if_possible`. This signals the `G1ConcurrentMarkThread` (in `g1ConcurrentMarkThread.cpp`) to begin a new marking cycle. The cycle starts with `G1ConcurrentMark::concurrent_cycle_start()` followed by `G1ConcurrentMark::mark_from_roots()` which does the actual concurrent tracing. The `G1ConcurrentMark` object itself is constructed and initialized in `g1CollectedHeap.cpp`. VM operations for initiating concurrent marking safepoints are in `g1VMOperations.cpp`.\n\n## 2. SATB Marking Queue Processing\n\nThe Snapshot-At-The-Beginning (SATB) write barrier is implemented across:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSet.cpp` — `G1BarrierSet::write_ref_array_pre_work()` and related methods intercept reference writes. When the SATB queue is active during concurrent marking, the pre-value of the overwritten reference is enqueued into the thread-local `SATBMarkQueue`.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1BarrierSetRuntime.cpp` — runtime stub `write_ref_field_pre_entry()` for compiled code paths that invokes `enqueue_known_active()` to log the old reference value.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/shared/satbMarkQueue.cpp` — base `SATBMarkQueue` and `SATBMarkQueueSet` classes. Buffers fill up during mutation and become 'completed buffers' for concurrent processing.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1SATBMarkQueueSet.cpp` — `G1SATBMarkQueueSet` extends the base with G1-specific filtering (`G1SATBMarkQueueFilterFn`) to exclude already-marked or young-gen references. During the Remark STW pause, `g1ConcurrentMark.cpp` contains `G1RemarkThreadsClosure` which flushes per-thread SATB queues so all remaining pre-values are processed before marking concludes.\n\n## 3. Remembered Set Scanning\n\nThe remembered set (remset) infrastructure is implemented in:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSet.cpp` — central `G1RemSet` class. `prepare_for_scan_heap_roots()` initializes the `G1RemSetScanState`; `scan_heap_roots()` iterates dirty card ranges and scans for cross-region references pointing into the collection set. The `G1RemSetScanState` inner class manages which regions have dirty cards to scan.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1HeapRegionRemSet.cpp` — per-region remembered set (`G1HeapRegionRemSet`) that tracks which other regions have pointers into a given region.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CardSet.cpp` — the card-set data structure (`G1CardSet`) backing each region's remembered set using a HOWL (Hierarchical Or With Lists) structure.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1RemSetTrackingPolicy.cpp` — decides which regions need remset tracking (`update_at_allocate`, `update_at_free`).\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1ConcurrentRebuildAndScrub.cpp` — concurrently rebuilds and scrubs remembered sets after a marking cycle completes.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1DirtyCardQueue.cpp` — the dirty card queue that feeds concurrent refinement threads (`g1ConcurrentRefine.cpp`) which keep remsets up to date between GC pauses.\n\n## 4. Mixed Collection Region Selection\n\nAfter concurrent marking completes, old-gen regions become candidates for mixed GC collections:\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetChooser.cpp` — `G1CollectionSetChooser::build()` is invoked post-marking to scan all old-gen regions, compute reclaimable bytes from the marking liveness data, sort regions by GC efficiency (reclaimable bytes per cost), and populate `G1CollectionSetCandidates`.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSetCandidates.cpp` — `G1CollectionSetCandidates` maintains the sorted list of candidate old-gen regions across marking groups and retained regions, and tracks membership in the collection set.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1CollectionSet.cpp` — `G1CollectionSet::finalize_old_part()` selects the actual set of old-gen regions for a mixed GC pause, constrained by the remaining pause time budget, pulling from candidates in efficiency order.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1YoungCollector.cpp` — `calculate_collection_set()` orchestrates collection set construction calling `finalize_initial_collection_set()` which in turn calls `finalize_old_part()` for mixed GC pauses.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1YoungGCPostEvacuateTasks.cpp` — post-GC tasks handle retained regions and re-sort candidates after each mixed collection.\n- `src/hotspot/share/gc/g1/g1Policy.cpp` — `G1Policy::candidates()` exposes the collection set candidates, and policy logic in `about_to_start_mixed_phase()` gates transition into the mixed collection phase."
